summary: Adherence retro for the PR #25 server-side session-storage run. Outcome was correct and verified (63 unit, 17+17 e2e, CI green, 5 sessions migrated). Adherence was partial: two chain steps ran in-process without a Skill-tool invocation, and the coding-agent subagent over-reached into the project-state-updater step.

observed_chain: planning-agent (in-process; artifact docs/ai/current-plan.md written; Skill NOT invoked)
acceptance-contract (in-process; failing contract tests written red-first; Skill NOT invoked)

issues.description: planning-agent and acceptance-contract were executed in-process and announced in prose ('Using planning-agent...', 'acceptance-contract / TDD') but the Skill tool was never invoked for them. Guard 5 requires in-process chain steps to still invoke the Skill (verification-gate and project-state-updater WERE invoked correctly, so the gap is inconsistent application, not total bypass). Artifacts were produced, so impact was low this run.

The dispatched coding-agent subagent updated docs/ai/project-state.md, decisions.md, repo-map.md, and testing-guide.md (the project-state-updater step) BEFORE verification-gate ran, and returned a memory-update summary instead of the explicitly-requested validation report (commands run + pass/fail). The orchestrator had to independently re-run all checks (correct per verification-gate discipline) and re-do project-state-updater after verification. coding-agent should report validation evidence and NOT perform the downstream memory-update step.

Host (Claude Code session) exposed no TodoWrite tool; the orchestrator and downstream skills repeatedly instruct 'expand into TodoWrite'. Checklist was tracked in docs/ai/current-plan.md instead. Minor; skills could acknowledge a no-TodoWrite fallback once.
